description_long: This object is a butterfly knife, also known as a balisong, featuring a sleek, single-edged blade with a sharp point and a straight profile. The blade is attached to a pair of symmetrical handles, each perforated with a series of circular holes for reduced weight and improved grip. The handles are joined by a pivot mechanism, allowing them to rotate around the blade for opening and closing. The entire knife has a dark, metallic finish, giving it a modern and tactical appearance. The construction appears to be primarily metal, likely stainless steel, with a smooth, glossy surface. No decorative engravings or ornamentation are visible, emphasizing a utilitarian design.
description: A dark, metallic butterfly knife with a straight, single-edged blade and perforated dual handles, designed for smooth rotation and grip.
description_view_0: The knife is viewed edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the closed alignment of the handles.
description_view_1: The knife is seen from the side, displaying the full length of the blade and the perforated handles in an open position.
description_view_2: The knife is viewed from the opposite edge, again showing the slim profile and the gap between the two handles.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the blade and handles, mirroring the features seen in view 1.
